commentaries.author: Victorinus of Pettau
commentaries.content: To be sweet in the mouth is the reward of the preaching of the speaker, and is most pleasant to the hearers; but it is most bitter both to those that announce it, and to those that persevere in its commandments through suffering.

commentaries.author: Victorinus of Pettau
commentaries.content: To take the book and eat it up, is, when exhibition of a thing is made to one, to commit it to memory.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
